text:
Australian football fans have woken to the heartbreaking news that Sam Kerr has suffered a "devastating" injury that has forced the 30-year-old out of the Olympic qualifiers.

Australian football hero Sam Kerr has suffered a horrific injury in training, putting her in doubt to compete for the Matildas at the Paris Olympic Games in six months' time.

The Matildas captain ruptured an anterior cruciate ligament, also known as an ACL, at a training camp over the weekend, her club Chelsea confirmed on Monday morning.

She was at the Women's Super League leaders' warm weather training in Morocco with Chelsea when she sustained the ACL injury, the second one since 2011.

It means the 30-year-old will be forced out of the remainder of the season with Chelsea and will not be able to play in the Olympic qualifiers against Uzbekistan next month.

Matilda's coach Tony Gustavsson fears the loss of Kerr through the shocking injury could impact their chances at making the Paris Games in July.

"Considering how hard Sam has worked over the past six months to return to play, this news is a devastating blow for everyone," he said in a statement.

"With her ability to lead by example, Sam's guidance and influence on the team is significant and, as a result, this will be an incredible loss for the national team."

Gustavsson said the focus will turn to Kerr's recovery and rehabilitation, which will be carefully undertaken by Chelsea medical staff.

Keep your head up,@SamKerr1.Weâ€™re all with you. ðŸ’™pic.twitter.com/m4m2U3K0Rc

It will be touch and go for the Matildas star to compete at the Olympics, as ACL injuries take at least six months but more closer to nine months to fully recover.

However, it may take even longer for professional athletes as doctors are cautious and prefer the injury to be completely healed before making a return to competition.

A timeframe is too early to be determined for Kerr.

The forward had recently suffered a calf injury and missed out on the first two games of the 2023 Women's World Cup, before returning in fine form to take Australia into the semi-finals which was watched by 11.5 million viewers in the country at its peak.

Australia is set to play a two-legged Olympic qualifying play-offs against Uzbekistan away on February 24 and then at Marvel Stadium in Melbourne on February 28.

The Matildas are favourites to win both matches, with their last meeting with the Asian country back in 2007 where Australia comfortably won 10-0.

The football tournament in France begins July 25 to August 10.
